name: -b
--baseURL
options.description: Hostname (and path) to the root, e.g. http://spf13.com/

name: -D
--buildDrafts
options.description: Include content marked as draft (default false)

name: -F
--buildFuture
options.description: Include content with publishdate in the future (default false)

options.name: --cacheDir
options.description: Filesystem path to cache directory. Defaults: $TMPDIR/hugo_cache/

options.name: --cleanDestinationDir
options.description: Remove files from destination not found in static directories (default false)

name: -d
--destination
options.description: Filesystem path to write files to

options.name: --disableKinds
options.description: Disable different kind of pages (home, RSS etc.)

options.name: --enableGitInfo
options.description: Add Git revision, date and author info to the pages (default false)

options.name: --forceSyncStatic
options.description: Copy all files when static is changed (default false)

options.name: --gc
options.description: Enable to run some cleanup tasks (remove unused cache files) after the build (default false)

options.name: --minify
options.description: Minify any supported output format (HTML, XML etc.) (default false)

options.name: --noChmod
options.description: Don't sync permission mode of files (default false)

options.name: --noTimes
options.description: Don't sync modification time of files (default false)

options.name: --path-warnings
options.description: Print warnings on duplicate target paths etc (default false)

options.name: --poll
options.description: Set this to a poll interval, e.g --poll 700ms, to use a poll based approach to watch for file system changes

options.name: --print-mem
options.description: Print memory usage to screen at intervals (default false)

options.name: --templateMetrics
options.description: Display metrics about template executions (default false)

options.name: --templateMetricsHints
options.description: Calculate some improvement hints when combined with --templateMetrics (default false)

options.name: --trace
options.description: Write trace to file (not useful in general)

options.name: --ignoreVendorPaths
options.description: Ignores any _vendor for module paths matching the given Glob pattern

options.name: --logFile
options.description: Log File path (if set, logging enabled automatically)

name: -s
--source
options.description: Filesystem path to read files relative from

name: -w
--watch
options.description: Watch filesystem for changes and recreate as needed (default true)

options.name: --renderToMemory
options.description: Render to memory (only useful for benchmark testing)

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Delete the Hugo Module cache for the current project

options.name: --pattern
options.description: Pattern matching module paths to clean (all if not set)", "e.g. "**hugo*"

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Resolves dependencies in your current Hugo Project

options.name: -d
options.description: Download packages only and do not install

options.name: -f
options.description: Valid only when -u is set, forces get -u not to verify that each package has been checked out from the source control repository implied by its import path. This can be useful if the source is a local fork of the original

options.name: -fix
options.description: Run the fix tool on downloaded packages before resolving dependencies or building the code

options.name: -insecure
options.description: Permits fetching from repositories and resolving custom domains using insecure schemes such as HTTP. Use with caution

options.name: -t
options.description: Also download the packages required to build the tests for the specified packages

options.name: -u
options.description: Recursively update modules. Use network to update the named packages and their dependencies. By default, get uses the network to check out missing packages but does not use it to look for updates to existing packages

options.name: -v
options.description: Enables verbose progress and debug output

subcommands.name: graph
subcommands.description: Use `hugo mod graph` from the relevant module directory and it will print the dependency graph, including vendoring, module replacement or disabled status

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize this project as a Hugo Module

subcommands.name: npm
subcommands.description: Various npm helpers

subcommands.name: pack
subcommands.description: Experimental: Prepares and writes a composite package.json file for your project

subcommands.name: tidy
subcommands.description: Remove unused entries in go.mod and go.sum

subcommands.name: vendor
subcommands.description: Vendor all module dependencies into the _vendor directory

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.description: Verify dependencies

options.name: --clean
options.description: Delete module cache for dependencies that fail verification

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Create new content for your site

name: -w
--watch
options.description: Watch filesystem for changes and recreate as needed (default true)

options.name: --bind
options.description: Interface to which the server will bind (default '127.0.0.1')

options.name: --disableBrowserError
options.description: Do not show build errors in the browser (default false)

options.name: --disableFastRender
options.description: Enables full re-renders on changes (default false)

options.name: --disableLiveReload
options.description: Watch without enabling live browser reload on rebuild (default false)

options.name: --liveReloadPort
options.description: Port for live reloading (i.e. 443 in HTTPS proxy situations) (default -1)

options.name: --meminterval
options.description: Interval to poll memory usage (requires --memstats), valid time units are 'ns', 'us' (or 'µs'), 'ms', 's', 'm', 'h'. (default '100ms')

options.name: --memstats
options.description: Log memory usage to this file

options.name: --navigateToChanged
options.description: Navigate to changed content file on live browser reload (default false)

name: -p
--port
options.description: Port on which the server will listen (default 1313)

options.name: --renderToDisk
options.description: Render to Destination path (default is false: render to memory & serve from there)
